{{short description|Men's association football team}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|São Tomé and Príncipe women's national football team}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=June 2021}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name              = São Tomé and Príncipe
| Badge             = 
| Nickname          = {{lang|pt|Seleção dos Falcões e Papagaios}} <br /> (Falcons and True Parrots Team) <br /> {{lang|pt|Guerreiros do Equador}} <br /> (Warriors of the [[Equator]]) <br /> {{lang|pt|Os Elefantes}} <br /> (The [[African forest elephant|Elephants]])
| Association       = [[São Toméan Football Federation|Federação Santomense de Futebol]] (FSF)
| Sub-confederation = [[Central African Football Federations' Union|UNIFFAC]] (Central Africa)
| Confederation     = [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] (Africa)
| Coach             = [[Ricardo Monsanto]]
| Captain           = [[Luís Leal (footballer, born 1987)|Luís Leal]]
| Most caps         = [[Joazhifel Soares]] (36)
| Top scorer        = [[Luís Leal (footballer, born 1987)|Luís Leal]] (11)
| Home Stadium      = [[Estádio Nacional 12 de Julho]]
| FIFA Trigramme    = STP
| FIFA Rank         = {{FIFA World Rankings|STP}}
| FIFA max          = 115
| FIFA max date     = March 2012
| FIFA min          = 200
| FIFA min date     = September – October 2007
| Elo Rank          = {{World Football Elo Ratings|São Tomé and Príncipe}}
| Elo max           = 166
| Elo max date      = June 1976
| Elo min           = 194
| Elo min date      = August 1998
| pattern_la1       =_stp24a 
| pattern_b1        =_stp24a
| pattern_ra1       =_stp24a 
| pattern_sh1       =_redbottom
| pattern_so1       =
| leftarm1          = FFFF00
| body1             = FFFF00
| rightarm1         = FFFF00
| shorts1           = 008000
| socks1            = FFFF00
| pattern_la2       =_stp24h
| pattern_b2        =_stp24h
| pattern_ra2       =_stp24h
| pattern_sh2       =_redbottom
| leftarm2          = 008000
| body2             = 008000
| rightarm2         = 008000
| shorts2           = FFFF00
| socks2            = 008000
| First game        = {{fb|GAB}} 6–1 {{fb-rt|STP}} <br /> ([[Libreville]], [[Gabon]]; 2 May 1976)
| Largest win       = {{nowrap|{{fb|STP}} 2–0 {{fb-rt|EQG}} <br /> ([[Libreville]], Gabon; 14 November 1999) <br /> {{fb|STP}} 2–0 {{fb-rt|SLE}} <br /> ([[São Tomé]], [[São Tomé and Príncipe]]; 8 April 2000) <br /> {{fb|MRI}} 1–3 {{fb-rt|STP}} <br /> ([[Belle Vue Harel]], [[Mauritius]]; 9 October 2019)}}
| Largest loss      = {{fb|CGO|1970}} 11–0 {{fb-rt|STP}} <br /> (Libreville, Gabon; 7 July 1976)
}}

The '''São Tomé and Príncipe national football team''' is the national [[association football]] team of [[São Tomé and Príncipe]] and is controlled by the [[São Toméan Football Federation]]. It is a member of the [[Confederation of African Football]] (CAF) and [[FIFA]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/17293721 |title=BBC Sport − São Tomé e Príncipe rocket up Fifa rankings |website=bbc.co.uk |publisher=BBC |date=7 March 2012 |access-date=3 December 2013 |archive-date=17 October 2013 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131017091930/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/football/17293721 |url-status=live }}</ref>

==History==
São Tomé and Príncipe's first ever match was a friendly against [[Gabon national football team|Gabon]] in May 1976. They lost by a score of 6–1. Their next game, during the 1976 [[Central African Games]], was a horrendous 11–0 loss to [[Congo national football team|Congo]], São Tomé's largest loss to date. São Tomé rounded off the competition with a 2–1 loss to [[Central African Republic national football team|Central African Republic]] and a 5–0 loss to [[Chad national football team|Chad]].

The following year, São Tomé picked up their first win, in a friendly versus [[Rwanda national football team|Rwanda]]. In both 1978 and 1987 they achieved a draw at home to [[Angola national football team|Angola]].

The Green and Yellows took an eleven year break, before a string of matches including their first entry to a [[2000 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|FIFA sanctioned tournament]]. At the [[UNIFAC Cup]] in 1999, they achieved their second win, 2–0 against [[Equatorial Guinea national football team|Equatorial Guinea]]. They won the next game after that, against [[Sierra Leone national football team|Sierra Leone]], 2–0. This two-in-a-row streak accompanied with a draw a few matches later placed them at their highest FIFA ranking to date, 179.

In 2003, São Tomé lost to [[Libya national football team|Libya]] 1–0 and 8–0, which was a major blow to their previous success.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.worldsoccer.com/blogs/sao-tome-e-principe-are-back-if-not-with-a-bang |title=São Tomé e Príncipe spring an unlikely surprise − World Soccer |publisher=Time Inc. UK |website=worldsoccer.com |date=12 February 2012 |access-date=3 December 2013 |archive-date=15 February 2012 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120215231656/http://www.worldsoccer.com/blogs/sao-tome-e-principe-are-back-if-not-with-a-bang |url-status=dead }}</ref> São Tomé did not participate in the qualification for the [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010 World Cup]], withdrawing before their first match, leaving them unranked in the [[FIFA World Rankings|FIFA rankings]] because they did not play any matches for four years.

On 11 November 2011, after an eight-year hiatus, São Tomé and Príncipe participated in the qualification for the [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014 World Cup]], losing to Congo 5–0, then drawing 1–1 with the same team four days later. São Tomé were reinstated in the FIFA rankings on 23 November 2011, entering at number 192.

In January 2012, in the preliminary round of [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2013 AFCON Qualifiers]], São Tomé achieved their first ever aggregate win, defeating [[Lesotho national football team|Lesotho]] 1–0 at home then successfully defending a 0–0 draw in [[Maseru]] seven days later. In the next round, São Tomé only narrowly lost 5–4 on aggregate to Sierra Leone. In the following years, São Tomé continued to show promise with impressive wins at home to [[Ethiopia national football team|Ethiopia]] and [[Libya national football team|Libya]] but poor away results prevented them from advancing again.

On 9 October 2019, São Tomé defeated [[Mauritius national football team|Mauritius]] 1–3 away from home in the first leg of their preliminary round tie of [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2021 AFCON Qualifiers]]. This was São Tomé and Príncipe's first away win in a competitive match ever. Four days later, São Tomé won 2–1 at home to advance 5–2 on aggregate and enter [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations qualification Group C|Group C]], facing [[Ghana national football team|Ghana]], [[South Africa national football team|South Africa]] and [[Sudan national football team|Sudan]], where they finished bottom with zero points.

During the [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations qualification preliminary round|2023 AFCON qualifiers]], São Tomé faced [[Mauritius national football team|Mauritius]], winning the first leg 1–0 before drawing the second leg 3–3 to advance to the group stages. Following the fixtures CAF sanctioned São Tomé for their first leg victory as they hadn't followed COVID protocol for [[Luís Leal (footballer, born 1987)|Luis Leal]], giving Mauritius a 3–0 victory and eliminating São Tomé from the competition.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Warshaw |first1=Andrew |title=Mauritius win 2023 AFCON qualifier slot after Sao Tome break covid rules |url=https://www.insideworldfootball.com/2022/05/17/mauritius-win-2023-afcon-qualifier-slot-sao-tome-break-covid-rules/ |website=Inside World Football |date=17 May 2022 |access-date=30 May 2022 |archive-date=29 June 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220629030534/https://www.insideworldfootball.com/2022/05/17/mauritius-win-2023-afcon-qualifier-slot-sao-tome-break-covid-rules/ |url-status=live }}</ref> São Tomé appealed the decision and, following an initial unsuccessful ruling, they were reinstated by CAF a week before the first qualifying group game.<ref>{{Cite news |last1=Shehu |first1=Idris |title=Mauritius out as CAF reinstates Sao Tome to Nigeria's group |url=https://www.thecable.ng/afcon-qualifiers-mauritius-out-as-caf-reinstates-sao-tome-to-nigerias-group |newspaper=[[TheCable]]|date=June 2022 |access-date=9 June 2022 |archive-date=9 June 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220609175510/https://www.thecable.ng/afcon-qualifiers-mauritius-out-as-caf-reinstates-sao-tome-to-nigerias-group |url-status=live }}</ref>

==Results and fixtures==
{{main|São Tomé and Príncipe national football team results}}
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{Football box collapsible
|format =
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group H#STP v EQG|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 4 September
|time       = {{UTZ|17:00|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|STP}}
|score      = 2–3
|team2      = {{fb|EQG}}
|goals1     =
*[[Ronaldo Afonso|Afonso]] {{goal|8|pen.|42|pen.}}
|goals2     =
*[[Pablo Ganet|Ganet]] {{goal|52}}
*[[Iban Salvador|Salvador]] {{goal|61}}
*[[José Nabil Ondo|Nabil]] {{goal|69}}
|stadium    = [[Honor Stadium]]
|location   = [[Oujda]], Morocco
|attendance =
|referee    = Mohamed Marouf ([[Egyptian Football Association|Egypt]])
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400019709
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format =
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group H#NAM v STP|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 8 September
|time       = {{UTZ|15:00|2}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|NAM}}
|score      = 3–0
|team2      = {{fb|STP}}
|goals1     = [[Peter Shalulile|Shalulile]] {{goal|41||66||71}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Obed Itani Chilume Stadium]]
|location   = [[Francistown]], Botswana
|attendance =
|referee    = Brahamou Sadou Ali ([[Nigerien Football Federation|Niger]])
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020807
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format =
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group H#STP v TUN|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 10 October
|time       = {{UTZ|17:00|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|STP}}
|score      = 0–6
|team2      = {{fb|TUN}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     = 
*[[Firas Chaouat|Chaouat]] {{goal|36}}
*[[Elias Saad|Saad]] {{goal|39||43}}
*[[Ismaël Gharbi|Gharbi]] {{goal|47}}
*[[Mohamed Ali Ben Romdhane|Ben Romdhane]] {{goal|68|pen.|90}}
|stadium    = [[Hammadi Agrebi Stadium]]
|location   = [[Tunis]], Tunisia
|attendance = 5,000
|referee    = Andofetra Rakotojaona ([[Malagasy Football Federation|Madagascar]])
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021071
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format =
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group H#STP v MWI|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 13 October
|time       = {{UTZ|14:00|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|STP}}
|score      = 1–0
|team2      = {{fb|MWI}}
|goals1     =
*[[Ronaldo Afonso|Afonso]] {{goal|62|pen.}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Sousse Olympic Stadium]]
|location   = [[Sousse]], Tunisia
|attendance =
|referee    = Jean Pierre Nguiene ([[Congolese Football Federation|Congo]])
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021075
|result     = W
}}

===2026===
{{Football box collapsible
|format =
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification preliminary round|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = 27 March
|time       = {{UTZ|17:00|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|STP}}
|score      = 0–3
|team2      = {{fb|ETH}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     = 
*[[Kenean Markneh|Markneh]] {{goal|23}}
*[[Abel Yalew|Yalew]] {{goal|32||78}}
|stadium    = [[Ben M'Hamed El Abdi Stadium]]
|location   = [[El Jadida]], Morocco
|attendance =
|referee    = El Hadji Amadou Sy ([[Senegalese Football Federation|Senegal]])
|report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-27/sao-tome-e-principe-vs-ethiopia/4048701
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format =
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification preliminary round|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = 31 March
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|3}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|ETH}}
|score      = 1–0
|team2      = {{fb|STP}}
|goals1     =
*[[Chernet Gugesa|Gugesa]] {{goal|7}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Dire Dawa Stadium]]
|location   = [[Dire Dawa]], Ethiopia
|attendance = 
|referee    = Mohamed Marouf ([[Egyptian Football Association|Egypt]])
|report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-31/ethiopia-vs-sao-tome-e-principe/4048702
|result     = L
}}

==Coaching staff==

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Role
! Name
|-
| Head coach
| {{flagicon|POR}} [[Ricardo Monsanto]]
|-
| Assistant coach
| {{flagicon|STP}} [[Vladimir Viegas]]
|-
| Assistant coach
| {{flagicon|POR}} [[Ricardo Vaz Reis]]
|-
| Fitness coach
| {{flagicon|STP}} [[William Barbosa (footballer, born 1983)|William Barbosa]]
|-
| Physiotherapist
| {{flagicon|STP}} [[Holiday Cruz]]
|}

==Coaching history==
{{div col|colwidth=22em}}
*{{flagicon|FRG}} [[Rudi Gutendorf]] (1984)
*{{flagicon|STP}} [[Juvenal Correia]] (1998)
*{{flagicon|STP}} [[Eduardo de Sousa (football manager)|Eduardo de Sousa]] (2000)
*{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Antônio Dumas]] (2000–2001)
*{{flagicon|POR}} [[Jose Ferraz]] (2003–2005)
*{{flagicon|STP}} [[Osvaldo Lima]] (2011)
*{{flagicon|CMR}} [[Gustave Clément Nyoumba]] (2011–2015)
*{{flagicon|STP}} [[António do Rosário]] (2015–2017)
*{{flagicon|CMR}} [[Gustave Clément Nyoumba]] (2017–2019)
*{{flagicon|STP}} [[Adriano Eusébio]] (2019–2024)
*{{flagicon|POR}} [[Ricardo Monsanto]] (2024–present)
{{div col end}}

==Players==

===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification]] preliminary round matches against [[Ethiopia national football team|Ethiopia]] on 27 and 31 March 2026.<ref>[https://www.stp-press.st/2026/03/04/sao-tome-e-principe-vai-defrontar-etiopia-para-can-2027/ STP Press]</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 31 March 2026, after the match against Ethiopia''

{{nat fs g start}}

{{nat fs g player|pos=GK|name=[[Pedro Mateus]]|age={{bda|df=y|1996|9|27}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[Karvan İK]]|clubnat=AZE}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=GK|name=Yaniel Bonfim|age={{bda|df=y|2004|11|2}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=Celtic Castilla|clubnat=ESP}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=GK|name=Saiel Ramos|age={{bda|df=y|1990|03|11}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Sport Operários e Benfica|Operários]]|clubnat=STP}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=DF|name=[[Vavá Pequeno]]|age={{bda|df=y|1994|2|5}}|caps=19|goals=0|club=[[Sport Grupo Sacavenense|Sacavenense]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=DF|name=[[Adjeil Neves]]|age={{bda|df=y|1996|10|7}}|caps=17|goals=0|club=[[OFC Spartak Pleven]]|clubnat=BUL}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=DF|name=[[Rogério Fernandes]]|age={{bda|df=y|2002|8|28}}|caps=15|goals=0|club=[[Futebol Clube de Oliveira do Hospital|Oliveira do Hospital]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=DF|name=Adjakson Ramos|age={{bda|df=y|2002|10|8}}|caps=15|goals=0|club=[[SV Tasmania Berlin]]|clubnat=GER}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=DF|name=Gilberto d'Almeida|age={{bda|df=y|2000|12|2}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[6 de Setembro]]|clubnat=STP}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=DF|name=[[Ricardo Fernandes (footballer, born 2002)|Ricardo Fernandes]]|age={{bda|df=y|2002|8|28}}|caps=11|goals=0|club=[[Futebol Clube de Oliveira do Hospital|Oliveira do Hospital]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=MF|name=[[Joel Neves]]|age={{bda|df=y|1996|5|1}}|caps=20|goals=0|club=[[Clube Oriental de Lisboa|Oriental]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=MF|name=Nicola Bragança|age={{bda|df=y|2000|4|23}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[Juventude Sport Clube|Juventude de Évora]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=MF|name=Waldimilson Santo|age={{bda|df=y|1997|6|1}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=Associação Murteirense|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=MF|name=[[Paulo Lima (footballer, born 1998)|Paulo Lima]]|age={{bda|df=y|1998|6|6}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Vaasan Palloseura|VPS]]|clubnat=FIN}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=FW|name=[[Luís Leal (footballer, born 1987)|Luís Leal]]|age={{bda|df=y|1987|5|29}}|caps=29|goals=11|club=Angizia Luco|clubnat=ITA}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=FW|name=[[Ronaldo Afonso|Ronaldo Lumungo]]|age={{bda|df=y|2001|7|11}}|caps=19|goals=3|club=[[F.C. Paços de Ferreira]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=FW|name=[[Marcos Barbeiro]]|age={{bda|df=y|1995|7|29}}|caps=19|goals=1|club=[[Atlético Clube da Malveira|Atlético da Malveira]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=FW|name=[[Ricardo Cardoso (footballer)|Ricardo Cardoso]]|age={{bda|df=y|2001|9|23}}|caps=14|goals=2|club=[[US Affrico]]|clubnat=ITA}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=FW|name=Sérgio Malé|age={{bda|df=y|2004|5|8}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[S.U. 1º Dezembro]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=FW|name=[[Kelve Semedo]]|age={{bda|df=y|2004|5|9}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[Centro Desportivo de Fátima|CD Fátima]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=FW|name=Dola Costa|age={{bda|df=y|1999|10|11}}|caps=6|goals=1|club=[[Bairros Unidos FC]]|clubnat=STP}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=FW|name=[[Gil Carvalho]]|age={{bda|df=y|1997|2|25}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Hythe Town F.C.|Hythe Town]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=FW|name=Pedro Monteiro|age={{bda|df=y|1998|9|18}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=SL Cartaxo|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|pos=FW|name=Hélder Samuca|age={{bda|df=y|2006|6|8}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[C.F. Estrela da Amadora]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players were also named to a squad in the last 12 months.

{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Nuno Malheiro]]|age={{bda|df=y|1994|2|1}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Grindavík men's football|UMF Grindavík]]|clubnat=ISL|latest=v. {{Fb|Liberia}}, 9 June 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Aldair Santos|Aldair]]|age={{bda|df=y|1989|9|4}}|caps=14|goals=0|club=[[Royal Scup Dieleghem Jette|RSD Jette]]|clubnat=BEL|latest=v. {{Fb|Liberia}}, 9 June 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Denilson Silva|Denilson]]|age={{bda|df=y|1998|7|2}}|caps=8|goals=1|club=[[NK Omladinac Gornja Vrba|Omladinac Gornja Vrba]]|clubnat=CRO|latest=v. {{Fb|Liberia}}, 9 June 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Edmílson Viegas]]|age={{bda|df=y|1996|10|29}}|caps=9|goals=1|club=[[Íþróttafélagið Höttur]]|clubnat=ISL|latest=v. {{Fb|Liberia}}, 9 June 2024}}
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{updated|31 March 2026}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Sao Tome and Principe |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/160/Sao_Tome_And_Principe.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with São Tomé and Príncipe.''

===Most appearances===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:175px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|align=left|[[Joazhifel Soares]]
|36
|1
|2011–2023
|-
|2
|align=left|'''[[Luís Leal (footballer, born 1987)|Luís Leal]]'''
|29
|11
|2012–present
|-
|3
|align=left|[[Ivonaldo]]
|26
|0
|2014–2023
|-
|rowspan=2|4
|align=left|'''[[Joel Neves]]'''
|20
|0
|2020–present
|-
|align=left|[[Zé (footballer, born 1991)|Zé]]
|20
|3
|2011–2021
|-
|rowspan=3|6
|align=left|'''[[Ronaldo Afonso]]'''
|19
|3
|2017–present
|-
|align=left|'''[[Marcos Barbeiro]]'''
|19
|1
|2016–present
|-
|align=left|'''[[Vavá Pequeno]]'''
|19
|0
|2019–present
|-
|9
|align=left|[[:fr:|Francisco do Nascimento]]
|18
|0
|2011–2018
|-
|rowspan=3|10
|align=left|[[Harramiz]]
|17
|2
|2015–2023
|-
|align=left|'''[[Adjeil Neves]]'''
|17
|0
|2021–present
|-
|align=left|'''[[Eba Viegas]]'''
|17
|0
|2021–present
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===
[[File:Luís Leal en Newell's Old Boys.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Luís Leal is São Tomé and Príncipe's top scorer with 11 goals.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:175px;"|Player
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|align=left|'''[[Luís Leal (footballer, born 1987)|Luís Leal]]'''
|11
|29
|{{#expr:11/29 round 2}}
|2012–present
|-
|2
|align=left|[[Jair Nunes]]
|4
|12
|{{#expr:4/12 round 2}}
|2011–2016
|-
|rowspan=2|3
|align=left|'''[[Ronaldo Afonso]]'''
|3
|19
|{{#expr:3/19 round 2}}
|2017–present
|-
|align=left|[[Zé (footballer, born 1991)|Zé]]
|3
|20
|{{#expr:3/20 round 2}}
|2011–2021
|-
|rowspan=5|5
|align=left|[[Amilcar Ramos|Amilcar]]
|2
|3
|{{#expr:2/3 round 2}}
|2000
|-
|align=left|'''[[Ricardo Cardoso (footballer)|Ricardo Cardoso]]'''
|2
|14
|{{#expr:2/14 round 2}}
|2021–present
|-
|align=left|'''[[:pt:Jokceleny Carvalho|Iniesta]]'''
|2
|15
|{{#expr:2/15 round 2}}
|2017–present
|-
|align=left|[[Harramiz]]
|2
|17
|{{#expr:2/17 round 2}}
|2015–2023
|-
|}

==Competition records==

===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan=41|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]
|colspan=8 |''Part of {{fb|Portugal}}''
|colspan=6 |''Part of {{fb|Portugal}}''
|-
|[[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]] to [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]
|colspan=8 |''Not a FIFA member''
|colspan=6 |''Not a FIFA member''
|-
| {{flagicon|Italy}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
|colspan=8 |''Did not enter''
|colspan=6 |''Declined participation''

|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|2
|1
|0
|1
|2
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|9
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=4|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|1
|1
|1
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
|2
|1
|0
|1
|1
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|2
|0
|0
|2
|1
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|10
|1
|0
|9
|5
|26
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined'' 
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total
!-
!{{Tooltip|0/9|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
!-
!-
!-
!-
!-
!-
!20
!3
!1
!16
!10
!51
|}

===Africa Cup of Nations===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[Africa Cup of Nations]] record
! rowspan="41" width="1" |
!colspan=6|Qualification record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1957 Africa Cup of Nations|1957]]||rowspan=9 colspan=8|''Part of {{fb|Portugal}}''
|rowspan=9 colspan=6|''Part of {{fb|Portugal}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|United Arab Republic}} [[1959 African Cup of Nations|1959]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1962 Africa Cup of Nations|1962]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1963 Africa Cup of Nations|1963]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1965 Africa Cup of Nations|1965]]
|- 
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1968 Africa Cup of Nations|1968]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1970 Africa Cup of Nations|1970]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon|1961}} [[1972 Africa Cup of Nations|1972]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt|1972}} [[1974 Africa Cup of Nations|1974]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1975}} [[1976 Africa Cup of Nations|1976]]||rowspan=6 colspan=8|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|rowspan=6 colspan=6|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1978 Africa Cup of Nations|1978]]
|- 
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[1980 Africa Cup of Nations|1980]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Libya|1977}} [[1982 Africa Cup of Nations|1982]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[1984 Africa Cup of Nations|1984]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[1986 Africa Cup of Nations|1986]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[1988 Africa Cup of Nations|1988]]||colspan=8 rowspan=6|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6 rowspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[1990 Africa Cup of Nations|1990]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Senegal}} [[1992 Africa Cup of Nations|1992]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1994 Africa Cup of Nations|1994]]
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[1996 Africa Cup of Nations|1996]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Burkina Faso}} [[1998 Africa Cup of Nations|1998]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} {{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[2000 Africa Cup of Nations|2000]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|Mali}} [[2002 Africa Cup of Nations|2002]]
|2
|0
|1
|1
|2
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia}} [[2004 Africa Cup of Nations|2004]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2006 Africa Cup of Nations|2006]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|9
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[2008 Africa Cup of Nations|2008]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Angola}} [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations|2010]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} {{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2012 Africa Cup of Nations|2012]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|- 
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]]
|rowspan=7 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|4
|2
|1
|1
|5
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} [[2015 Africa Cup of Nations|2015]]
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]
|6
|1
|0
|5
|4
|19
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]
|2
|0
|0
|2
|2
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]]
|8
|2
|0
|6
|8
|18
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations|2023]]
|6
|1
|2
|3
|7
|22
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]]
|2
|0
|2
|0
|1
|1
|-
||{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations|2027]]
| colspan="8" rowspan="2" |''To be determined''
| colspan="6" rowspan="2" |''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|}} [[2029 Africa Cup of Nations|2029]]
|-
!'''Total'''
!'''0 titles'''
!'''0/35'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!36
!6
!6
!24
!29
!93
|}

==Head-to-head record==
{{#lst:São Tomé and Príncipe national football team results|Record by opponent}}

==References==
{{Reflist}}

==External links==
* [https://www.cafonline.com/member-associations/stp/ São Tomé and Príncipe] at [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/STP São Tomé and Príncipe] at [[FIFA]]

{{São Tomé and Príncipe national football team}}
{{Navboxes top
|bg = #009739
|fg = #FFD100
|bordercolor= #EF3340
|list1=}}
{{São Tomé and Príncipe national football team managers}}
{{Football in São Tomé and Príncipe}}
{{UNIFFAC football}}
{{CAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of São Tomé and Príncipe}}
{{Navboxes bottom}}
{{#invoke:Portal bar|main|Association football|São Tomé and Príncipe}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Sao Tome And Principe National Football Team}}
[[Category:São Tomé and Príncipe national football team| ]]
[[Category:African national association football teams]]
